questions.text: What makes things visible to us?
options: Reflection of light from objects into our eyes
questions.explanation: We can see objects only when light from them enters our eyes.

questions.text: The bouncing back of light when it strikes a shining surface is called:
questions.explanation: Reflection is the change in direction of light upon striking a surface.

questions.text: According to the Law of Reflection, the angle of incidence is ________ the angle of reflection.
questions.explanation: The first law of reflection states that ∠i = ∠r.

questions.text: The incident ray, the reflected ray, and the normal at the point of incidence lie in:
questions.explanation: This is the second law of reflection.

questions.text: In a plane mirror, the left side of the object appears to be the right side of the image. This is called:
questions.explanation: Lateral inversion is the side-to-side reversal in a plane mirror.

questions.text: The reflection from a smooth surface like a mirror is called:
questions.explanation: Regular reflection occurs when parallel rays strike a smooth surface and remain parallel.

questions.text: The reflection from a rough or uneven surface is called:
questions.explanation: Diffused reflection occurs when rays are reflected in different directions from a rough surface.

questions.text: Objects which shine in the light of other objects are called:
questions.explanation: Non-luminous objects seen due to light from others are illuminated objects.

questions.text: How many images are formed by two parallel plane mirrors facing each other?
questions.explanation: Parallel mirrors produce an infinite number of images.

questions.text: Which device works on the principle of multiple reflections and creates beautiful patterns?
questions.explanation: A kaleidoscope uses multiple mirrors to create symmetrical patterns.

questions.text: Which device is used in submarines to see objects on the surface of water?
questions.explanation: A periscope allows the user to see things otherwise out of the line of sight.

questions.text: Sunlight, also called white light, consists of ________ colors.
questions.explanation: White light is a mixture of seven colors (VIBGYOR).

questions.text: The splitting of white light into its constituent colors is called:
questions.explanation: Dispersion is the phenomenon that causes a rainbow.

questions.text: Which part of the eye gives it its distinctive color?
questions.explanation: The iris is the colored part of the eye that controls the pupil.

questions.text: The small opening in the iris that allows light to enter the eye is called:
questions.explanation: The pupil size changes to regulate the amount of light entering.

questions.text: On which part of the eye is the image formed?
questions.explanation: The retina acts as a screen where light sensitive cells are located.

questions.text: The nerve cells that are sensitive to bright light and color are:
questions.explanation: Cones detect color and work best in bright light.

questions.text: The nerve cells that are sensitive to dim light are:
questions.explanation: Rods are sensitive to low intensity light.

questions.text: The point in the eye where no vision is possible because of no sensory cells is called:
questions.explanation: The blind spot is the junction of the optic nerve and the retina.

questions.text: Persistence of vision for the human eye is about ________ of a second.
questions.explanation: The image stays on the retina for about 1/16th of a second.

questions.text: A person with ________ can see near objects clearly but not distant ones.
questions.explanation: Myopia is also known as short-sightedness.

questions.text: Cloudiness of the eye lens that occurs usually in old age is called:
questions.explanation: Cataract makes vision opaque and can be corrected by surgery.

questions.text: Which vitamin deficiency leads to night blindness?
questions.explanation: Vitamin A is essential for healthy eyesight.

questions.text: The most popular resource for visually challenged persons to read is:
questions.explanation: The Braille system uses raised dots for reading by touch.

questions.text: Louis Braille developed the Braille system in:
questions.explanation: He was himself a visually challenged person.

questions.text: The distance between the object and the mirror is ________ the distance between the mirror and the image.
questions.explanation: In a plane mirror, the image is as far behind as the object is in front.

questions.text: Eyes of which animal can see behind them even when looking forward?
questions.explanation: Crabs have eyes on stalks that provide a wide range of vision.

questions.text: An owl can see very well in the night but not during the day because:
options: It has more rods than cones
questions.explanation: Large number of rods help in seeing in faint light.

questions.text: When the angle between two mirrors is 90 degrees, how many images are formed?
A ray of light falling on a mirror is called:
questions.explanation: The ray that hits the surface is the incident ray.

questions.text: The normal is a line at ________ degrees to the reflecting surface.
questions.explanation: Normal is always perpendicular to the surface at the point of incidence.

questions.text: What type of image is formed by a plane mirror?
questions.explanation: Virtual images cannot be caught on a screen.

questions.text: Braille system has ________ dot patterns.
questions.explanation: There are 63 dot patterns or characters in Braille.

questions.text: The change in the size of the ________ regulates the light entering the eye.
questions.explanation: The iris contracts or expands to change pupil size.

questions.text: We see a rainbow because of:
questions.explanation: Raindrops act like prisms dispersing sunlight.

questions.text: Regular reflection is responsible for:
options: Formation of images in a mirror
questions.explanation: Only smooth surfaces that produce regular reflection form clear images.

questions.text: The visually challenged can use ________ to help them orient with their surroundings.
questions.explanation: Non-optical aids include auditory (sound) and tactual (touch) aids.

questions.text: If you stand 1 meter away from a mirror, how far is your image from you?
questions.explanation: 1m (you to mirror) + 1m (mirror to image) = 2m.

questions.text: Our eyes should be washed with ________ water for good health.
questions.explanation: Maintaining hygiene is key to preventing eye infections.

questions.text: To read comfortably, the normal distance for the eye is about ________ cm.
questions.explanation: 25 cm is the least distance of distinct vision for a normal human eye.
